text.title: Solo-Scale Affiliate Program Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Solo-Scale Affiliate Program. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Use platform built-ins; no custom referral code in v1. Custom = maintenance debt for solo founder.
rule.rationale: Custom builds eat months; payback never lands.

rule.statement: Commission % ≤ product margin %. Otherwise a referred sale loses money.
Attribution window in 30-90 days. Outside this range affiliates either churn (too short) or game (too long).
rule.rationale: Outside this window the program either under-pays or over-pays.

rule.statement: Self-referral, refund-clawback, payout-threshold rules declared on day 1 — not after the first dispute.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc rules invite disputes + cause refunds.

rule.statement: Named founder owner + per-period payout cap (e.g. $500/mo) until trust established.
rule.rationale: Uncapped payouts in a young program expose runway.

rule.statement: Commissions clawed back on refunds within refund_window_days. Disclosed in affiliate terms.
rule.rationale: Without clawback, refunds turn into commission leakage.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-affiliate-program-solo.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
